lede: A look at February 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Abraham Lincoln and Charles Darwin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: North Macedonia
events.desc: The country known as the Republic of Macedonia renames itself the Republic of North Macedonia in accordance with the Prespa agreement, settling a long-standing naming dispute with Greece.

events.title: San Francisco
events.desc: The city of San Francisco begins issuing marriage licenses to same-sex couples in response to a directive from Mayor Gavin Newsom.

events.title: Murder of James Bulger
events.desc: Two-year-old James Bulger is abducted from New Strand Shopping Centre by two ten-year-old boys, who later torture and murder him.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Deadlight ends after scuttling 121 of 154 captured U-boats.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of February 12, 1945
events.desc: A devastating tornado outbreak in Mississippi and Alabama kills 45 people and injures 427 others.

events.title: USS Macon (ZRS-5)
events.desc: USS Macon, one of the two largest helium-filled airships ever created, crashes into the Pacific Ocean off the coast of California and sinks.

events.title: SS Penguin
events.desc: New Zealand's worst maritime disaster of the 20th century happens when the SS Penguin, an inter-island ferry, sinks and explodes at the entrance to Wellington Harbour.

events.title: Argentina
events.desc: An Argentine/Chilean patriotic army, after crossing the Andes, defeats Spanish troops at the Battle of Chacabuco.

events.title: Isabella I of Castile
events.desc: Isabella I issues an edict outlawing Islam in the Crown of Castile, forcing virtually all her Muslim subjects to convert to Christianity.

births.name: Abraham Lincoln
births.line: American lawyer and statesman, 16th President of the United States

births.name: Charles Darwin
births.line: English naturalist, geologist, biologist and theorist

births.name: 13th Dalai Lama
births.line: Spiritual leader of Tibet from 1879 to 1933

deaths.text: Peter Alexander (Austrian performer), Austrian singer and actor
Jean Renoir, French actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Immanuel Kant, German anthropologist, philosopher, and academic
Adolf Frederick of Sweden, King of Sweden
Pierre de Marivaux, French author and playwright
